title: Galium langei
taxonomy: Galium langei is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Galium langei is classified in the genus Galium, which comprises about 400 species of plants commonly known as bedstraws. The plant is often found in dry to moist areas and can grow as a perennial or annual. Galium langei is considered a rare species in some areas due to habitat destruction and other environmental factors.

intro: Galium langei, also known as Lange's bedstraw, is a perennial plant species in the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its small, white or pinkish flowers and narrow, pointed leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Galium langei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Galium langei is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to leverage its therapeutic potential.
